commands: jq upstream handoff JSON files and ptarcade_bayes.json
warnings: Several upstream handoffs are warning-status but usable.
No new physics calculations or scans were performed.
Highest-severity issues are uncontrolled because the current workflow has not computed thermal history, relic abundance, RG validity, or full supercooling completion diagnostics.
approximations: Severity rankings are interpretive audit classifications, not new physics exclusions.
Controlled=true means the workflow either enforces the assumption or records an explicit applicability limitation; it does not mean the assumption is physically validated.
Required followups include only unresolved items that materially affect interpretation.

upstream_statuses.note: Usable proposal; no numerical FOPT/PTA claims.
Literature benchmark mapping caveats are explicit.
No red flags; warnings remain for exact-zero portals, beta_lambda positivity, and backend approximations.
PTArcade and benchmark outputs are usable with DBF-template and MAP-boundary caveats.
No non-PTA published constraint was applied as an exclusion; several constraints require new backend observables.
Bayes summary present; MAP has y_D=0 boundary.

assumptions.description: The backend fixes hypercharge-dark kinetic mixing exactly to epsilon=0, removing visible dark-photon production and decay channels from the analysis.
assumptions.required_solution: Either justify exact epsilon=0 as a protected model assumption or add a nonzero-epsilon portal/decay/thermalization scan and recast visible and invisible dark-photon constraints.

source.note: epsilon is fixed to zero although kinetic mixing is symmetry-allowed.

assumptions.description: The backend fixes the Higgs portal exactly to lambda_HP=0, removing scalar mixing, visible scalar decays, and SM thermal contact from the workflow.
assumptions.required_solution: Either justify exact lambda_HP=0 as part of the model definition or implement portal-dependent scalar mixing, decay widths, and thermalization checks.

source.note: lambda_HP is fixed to zero although the operator is symmetry-allowed.

assumptions.description: The PTArcade MAP estimate sits at the physical lower boundary y_D=0, so the posterior should not be interpreted as evidence for an interior Yukawa preference.
assumptions.required_solution: Report y_D as boundary-sensitive and rerun only if a different prior, one-sided interval treatment, or profile-likelihood boundary analysis is needed.

source.note: map_est y_D is 0.0 while the prior lower bound is 0.0.

assumptions.description: PTArcade uses a log-uniform prior for v_D over 10^-3.5 to 10 GeV and uniform priors for g_D and y_D; posterior summaries depend on this prior volume.
assumptions.required_solution: Perform prior-sensitivity reruns only if quantitative model comparison or robust parameter inference is required.

source.note: v_D sampled uniformly in log10_v_D; g_D and y_D sampled uniformly.

assumptions.description: The rectangular PTArcade 1-sigma region contains beta_lambda<0 corners, so rectangular mass ranges include unphysical Coleman-Weinberg points unless a beta_lambda>0 mask is enforced.
assumptions.required_solution: Apply the beta_lambda>0 mask directly to posterior samples and quote masked credible regions instead of independent rectangular corners.

source.note: The beta_lambda corner range includes a negative low value.

assumptions.description: The FOPT calculation uses the semianalytic conformal single-field small-field/high-temperature polynomial backend rather than a full finite-temperature effective potential and bounce pipeline.
assumptions.required_solution: Cross-check key posterior and benchmark points with a full finite-temperature potential and bounce/percolation calculation.

source.note: Small-field/high-temperature semianalytic backend is explicitly listed.

assumptions.description: Percolation and completion are treated with an approximate backend diagnostic, while several accepted points are strongly supercooled with very large alpha.
assumptions.required_solution: Implement robust completion, vacuum-domination, reheating, and wall-velocity diagnostics for the preferred region and 14/14 benchmark points.

source.note: Percolation and completion under strong supercooling is classified as approximate.

assumptions.description: The PTA analysis uses the DBF spectrum template as the closest supported template, but its validity is not strict for very strong or supercooled transitions.
assumptions.required_solution: Compare against templates or simulations valid for runaway/supercooled transitions, or explicitly restrict interpretation to qualitative PTA compatibility.

source.note: DBF template validity status is warning.

assumptions.description: No BBN/CMB/Delta N_eff thermal-history calculation is present for the zero-portal hidden sector, including temperature ratio, decoupling, abundances, or lifetimes.
assumptions.required_solution: Add a thermal-history module computing dark-sector temperature ratio, decoupling, entropy transfer, abundances, and lifetimes through BBN and recombination.

source.note: thermal_history_neff.py and bbn_cmb_injection.py are listed as high-priority missing modules.

assumptions.description: The workflow does not establish dark fermion stability or relic abundance, so dark-matter and CMB energy-injection interpretations remain unresolved.
assumptions.required_solution: Specify the stability symmetry and compute Boltzmann relic abundance, annihilation channels, and dark-sector temperature dependence.

source.note: Relic abundance requires a new backend.

assumptions.description: Velocity-dependent self-interaction constraints cannot be applied without a stable DM assignment, relic fraction, and transfer/viscosity cross sections.
assumptions.required_solution: Compute self-interaction cross sections over dwarf, Milky Way, and cluster velocity scales after fixing DM identity and abundance.

assumptions.description: No RG running, Landau-pole scale, loop-expansion stability, or scale-variation calculation was performed for the Coleman-Weinberg model.
assumptions.required_solution: Implement RG running and Landau-pole checks for g_D, y_D, and lambda_D over the quoted posterior region.

source.note: cw_rg_validity.py is listed as high priority.

assumptions.description: Literature benchmark mapping is approximate because source conventions differ in vector mass normalization, charge assignments, portals, kinetic mixing, Yukawa treatment, and RG scale.
assumptions.required_solution: Use benchmark points only as scan seeds unless each source model is translated into the target q_Phi=2, epsilon=0, lambda_HP=0 convention.

source.note: Convention and portal mismatches are repeatedly flagged.

assumptions.description: The FOPT is assumed to be controlled by a single radial Coleman-Weinberg order parameter, excluding multi-field portal dynamics or additional scalar directions.
assumptions.required_solution: No followup is required for the current branch unless portals or additional scalar fields are reintroduced.

source.note: Single radial background chi is part of the validated backend model.

assumptions.description: The backend compresses two degenerate Majorana Weyl eigenstates into one fermion species with dof=4; this is acceptable for FOPT bookkeeping but not a full dark-matter model.
assumptions.required_solution: Retain the explicit two-Weyl-field interpretation in downstream reports and avoid calling the species a Dirac fermion without extra structure.

source.note: Critic-agent fixed and documented the bookkeeping convention.

assumptions.description: Benchmark scoring uses tabulated PTA violin windows over 14 bins and a template-independent f_peak_est guide, not a full likelihood for every benchmark grid point.
assumptions.required_solution: Use PTArcade likelihood outputs for quantitative inference; treat 14-bin benchmark scores as supporting diagnostics.

source.note: Benchmark scoring uses pta_violin_windows.csv and DBF spectra.

assumptions.description: Explicit thermal-mass expressions in the model handoffs are placeholders and are not direct inputs to the semianalytic backend.
assumptions.required_solution: No immediate followup is required unless replacing the semianalytic backend with an explicit finite-temperature potential.

source.note: Thermal masses are marked analytic placeholder only.

assumptions.description: PTArcade completed N_samples=100000 but stored 10001 rows after thinning/storage; this is documented and not itself a failure.
assumptions.required_solution: No immediate followup is required unless a formal convergence diagnostic or independent rerun is requested.

source.note: Stored rows are consistent with PTArcade storage/thinning.

required_followups.description: Apply a beta_lambda>0 mask to posterior samples and recompute physical credible regions and derived mass ranges.
blocked_by: Access to final PTArcade posterior samples or chain parsing module
required_followups.suggested_action: Create a masked posterior summary script for v_D, g_D, y_D, m_X, m_psi, m_chi, alpha, beta/H, and Treh.

required_followups.description: Compute thermal history, BBN, CMB, and Delta N_eff observables for the exact-zero-portal hidden sector.
required_followups.suggested_action: Implement a thermal_history_neff.py module with decoupling, entropy transfer, species abundances, and BBN/recombination Delta N_eff checks.

required_followups.description: Cross-check supercooled preferred points with robust percolation, completion, reheating, wall-velocity, and GW-template validity diagnostics.
blocked_by: No full finite-temperature potential and bounce/percolation backend for this model
required_followups.suggested_action: Run a full FOPT calculation for MAP, masked posterior representatives, and top 14/14 benchmark points.

required_followups.description: Clarify whether epsilon=0 and lambda_HP=0 are exact protected model assumptions or backend conveniences.
blocked_by: No symmetry or UV argument for exact-zero portals in upstream handoffs
required_followups.suggested_action: Either document a protective symmetry/UV condition or add small nonzero portal priors and recast thermalization, decay, and laboratory constraints.

required_followups.description: Establish dark fermion stability and compute relic abundance, annihilation channels, and possible CMB energy-injection observables.
required_followups.suggested_action: Specify the stabilizing symmetry and implement dark_relic_density.py with the dark-sector temperature ratio as an input.

required_followups.description: Compute RG running, perturbativity, Landau-pole scale, and scale-variation uncertainty for the Coleman-Weinberg model.
blocked_by: No RG module or beta-function audit for the posterior region
required_followups.suggested_action: Implement cw_rg_validity.py and evaluate masked posterior samples.

required_followups.description: Compute velocity-dependent self-interaction constraints if the dark fermion is interpreted as dark matter.
required_followups.suggested_action: After relic-density resolution, calculate transfer or viscosity cross sections at dwarf, Milky Way, and cluster velocities.
